one_time_description: Settlement received in January 2020 from the Government of Canada related to the TopGas settlement claim
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Pimee Well Servicing Ltd. (17% ownership), Pimee Limited Partnership, Seven Lakes Oilfield Services Limited Partnership (8.33%), Akuna Drilling Trust, 1678902 Alberta Ltd. (100%), and Beaver Lake Cree Nation Holdings GP Inc. (100%). Oil and gas royalties and surface rights payments are administered by the Government of Canada under the Indian Oil and Gas Act.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (not 2024). Large one-time TopGas settlement of $2,974,794 included in Other Revenue significantly boosted surplus. Five entities consolidated: Beaver Lake Cree Nation government, Beaver Lake Education Authority - Amisk Community School, Beaver Lake Wah-Pow Treatment Centre, Beaver Lake Health Services, and Beaver Lake Intergovernmental Affairs and Industrial Relations. Long-term investments accounted for by modified equity method are not consolidated (Pimee Well Servicing, Pimee LP, Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP). COVID-19 pandemic noted as uncertainty. Restricted cash of $583,173 includes Province of Alberta ($332,304) and CMHC reserves ($250,869). No schedule of remuneration provided. ISC includes a $4,000,000 lagoon project funding largely deferred. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($6,970,039) + Health Canada ($3,563,034) + CMHC ($123,928) + HRDC ($555,501) = $11,212,502.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
